_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to analyse how language features shape meaning and vary depending on the audience and purpose of a text.

_source.w: We are learning to analyse how language features shape meaning and vary depending on the audience and purpose of a text.
y: Understanding how language creates meaning is the core skill of critical literacy.
Analysing language variation helps us read between the lines and evaluate texts effectively.
This skill helps us make more deliberate language choices in our own communication.
eg: We analyse how an author's word choices shape the reader's perception of a character or event.
We compare the language features of a text written for teenagers with one written for adults on the same topic.
We identify how language features like modality, evaluative language and sentence structure serve the text's purpose.
We discuss how the same information can be presented differently through language choices.
We evaluate whether an author's language choices effectively serve their purpose and audience.
We experiment with different language features in our own writing to achieve different effects.
